title: Ny techfrossa i Asien – blickarna vänds mot Volvo
summary_md:
# Ny techfrossa i Asien – blickarna vänds mot Volvo

**Feed**: efn-borsfrukost  
**Published**: 2026-06-10  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

A Swedish morning markets show (Börsfrukost) covering heavy volatility in Asian tech-heavy indices ahead of the SpaceX IPO, Volvo's capital markets day in Eskilstuna focused on Volvo CE, and defense names Kongsberg and Saab. The second half features Schroders EM manager Tom Wilson on extreme tech concentration (~43% of the EM benchmark, TSMC ~15%) and a derated India. Mood is mixed: constructive on quality industrials/defense and EM tech structurally, but wary of stretched valuations, geopolitical/inflation risk and a wave of IPO emissions draining liquidity.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟠 SYSR — Systemair | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Strong first report under new CEO, 9% organic growth, adj EBIT above expectations, optimistic outlook; hosts find it interesting but haven't bought.

#### Reasoning

Robert Larsson's debut report showed 9% organic growth and adjusted EBIT (~290m) ahead of expectations, with cautious optimism on Germany, Nordics and strong US, and little geopolitical impact. Hosts compare it favorably to Lindab and call it interesting but have not yet taken a position, awaiting confirmation; tariff-driven plan to shift production to USA is a watch item.

#### Sell Triggers

- if organic growth decelerates below mid-single digits
- if tariffs erode margins despite US production shift
- if European/German demand weakens

> *"System Air har vi faktiskt tittat på. Vi har inte köpt någonting än. Men jag tycker det är ett intressant bolag."*

---

### 🟡 VOLV-B — Volvo | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Capital markets day with VCE in focus; strong year, generous dividends, possible US guidance raise; hosts argue against splitting VCE now given EV/battery uncertainty.

#### Reasoning

Volvo's share has outperformed the index and pays large dividends. Hosts expect a constructive capital markets day centered on Volvo CE's quality shift, potential US truck guidance upgrade given strong ACT data, and possible extra dividend. They view a VCE spin-off as ill-timed because shared development of electric drivelines and batteries still benefits from keeping the businesses together.

#### Sell Triggers

- if truck cycle turns down in US/Europe
- if capital allocation disappoints
- if EV transition costs escalate

> *"Det ser ju ut att bli ännu ett väldigt bra år för Volvo naturligtvis."*

---

### 🟠 SAAB-B — Saab | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Defense sector pressured by AI-disruption fears, but hosts argue Saab-type firms are AI winners via faster software development; Kongsberg's ambitious targets read positively.

#### Reasoning

The defense sector has faced an 'AI threat' narrative, but hosts contend established players like Saab are net beneficiaries because AI lets them develop software faster. Kongsberg's aggressive growth targets (triple revenue by 2029) are seen as supportive for Saab. Stock only marginally up on the day.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI genuinely displaces incumbent defense software vendors
- if defense order intake slows
- if Kongsberg targets prove unattainable, denting sector sentiment

> *"Hittills så skulle jag gissa att de här SAAB-bolagen är egentligen vinnare på AI-trenden."*

---

### 🟠 KOG — Kongsberg | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Capital markets day unveiled very ambitious targets (3x revenue by 2029, 5x by 2033); stock up 6.3%; hosts note these imply huge capacity buildout.

#### Reasoning

Kongsberg set highly ambitious long-term targets to triple revenue by 2029 and quintuple by 2033, lifting the stock 6.3%. Hosts are impressed but skeptical about the feasibility of tripling production in four years, implying significant idle/new capacity needed. Positive read-across to Saab.

#### Sell Triggers

- if revenue ramp falls short of guided trajectory
- if capacity expansion overruns on cost
- if defense spending momentum fades

> *"De vill trippla omsättningen till 2029 och femdubblaren till 2033."*

---

### 🟢 TSM — TSMC | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Schroders EM manager: ~15% of benchmark, deep tech moat, dominates leading-edge nodes, wins regardless of Nvidia vs ASIC mix, disciplined capital allocation; high-quality core.

#### Reasoning

Tom Wilson frames TSMC as the highest-quality, lowest-risk constituent within the heavily concentrated EM tech complex: substantial technological moat, leading-edge node dominance, beneficiary whether demand comes from Nvidia GPUs or ASICs, and disciplined on capital allocation. It moves with the cycle but is structurally sound; Schroders runs a contained overweight in EM tech.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI capex cycle reverses
- if leading-edge competition erodes pricing
- if a 'DeepSeek-style' shock hits structural AI demand

> *"TSMC has very substantial technological mode and TSMC as a function of that dominates leading edge node"*

---

### 🟠 TSLA — Tesla | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 40%

Losing momentum as investors rotate capital toward the SpaceX bet on Musk; speculation Tesla could one day be combined with his space ventures.

#### Reasoning

Hosts note Tesla is losing steam because investors can't keep all their Musk exposure in Tesla and are reallocating to the upcoming SpaceX issuance. There is speculation Tesla could eventually be folded into Musk's broader vision. View is speculative and sentiment-driven rather than fundamental.

#### Sell Triggers

- if SpaceX IPO diverts sustained capital from Tesla
- if Tesla fundamentals deteriorate
- if Musk-narrative momentum reverses

> *"Tesla börjar tappa för att man tänker att man kan inte ha alla kanske pengar i Tesla."*

---
